title: “STdeconvolve” author: “yo” date: “2026-01-27” output: html_document —

##STdeconvolve

##Cargar librerías

library(STdeconvolve)
library(Matrix)
library(readr)
library(MERINGUE)
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'MERINGUE'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:STdeconvolve':
## 
##     cleanCounts, getOverdispersedGenes
library(SpatialExperiment)
## Cargando paquete requerido: SingleCellExperiment
## Cargando paquete requerido: SummarizedExperiment
## Cargando paquete requerido: MatrixGenerics
## Cargando paquete requerido: matrixStats
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'MatrixGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     colAlls, colAnyNAs, colAnys, colAvgsPerRowSet, colCollapse,
##     colCounts, colCummaxs, colCummins, colCumprods, colCumsums,
##     colDiffs, colIQRDiffs, colIQRs, colLogSumExps, colMadDiffs,
##     colMads, colMaxs, colMeans2, colMedians, colMins, colOrderStats,
##     colProds, colQuantiles, colRanges, colRanks, colSdDiffs, colSds,
##     colSums2, colTabulates, colVarDiffs, colVars, colWeightedMads,
##     colWeightedMeans, colWeightedMedians, colWeightedSds,
##     colWeightedVars, rowAlls, rowAnyNAs, rowAnys, rowAvgsPerColSet,
##     rowCollapse, rowCounts, rowCummaxs, rowCummins, rowCumprods,
##     rowCumsums, rowDiffs, rowIQRDiffs, rowIQRs, rowLogSumExps,
##     rowMadDiffs, rowMads, rowMaxs, rowMeans2, rowMedians, rowMins,
##     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks,
##     rowSdDiffs, rowSds, rowSums2, rowTabulates, rowVarDiffs, rowVars,
##     rowWeightedMads, rowWeightedMeans, rowWeightedMedians,
##     rowWeightedSds, rowWeightedVars
## Cargando paquete requerido: GenomicRanges
## Cargando paquete requerido: stats4
## Cargando paquete requerido: BiocGenerics
## Cargando paquete requerido: generics
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'generics'
## The following object is masked from 'package:MERINGUE':
## 
##     interpolate
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff,
##     setequal, union
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'BiocGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind,
##     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find,
##     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget,
##     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank,
##     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique,
##     unsplit, which.max, which.min
## Cargando paquete requerido: S4Vectors
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'S4Vectors'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:Matrix':
## 
##     expand, unname
## The following object is masked from 'package:utils':
## 
##     findMatches
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     expand.grid, I, unname
## Cargando paquete requerido: IRanges
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'IRanges'
## The following object is masked from 'package:grDevices':
## 
##     windows
## Cargando paquete requerido: Seqinfo
## Cargando paquete requerido: Biobase
## Welcome to Bioconductor
## 
##     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with
##     'browseVignettes()'. To cite Bioconductor, see
##     'citation("Biobase")', and for packages 'citation("pkgname")'.
## 
## Adjuntando el paquete: 'Biobase'
## The following object is masked from 'package:MatrixGenerics':
## 
##     rowMedians
## The following objects are masked from 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     anyMissing, rowMedians
filtered_spe_norm <- readRDS("C:/Users/ruben/Desktop/RUBEN/BBC_master/3_cuatri/TFM/Bioconductor/filtered_spe_norm.rds")

Cargar archivos

Primero es necesario cargar todas las variables necesarias del objeto de Spatial Experiment con el que hemos realizado el preprocesamiento de las muestras. Se va a aplicar la deconvolución a tres muestras distintas: ST_04, ST_01 y ST_16.

ST_04

# Ver las muestras disponibles en el objeto
unique(filtered_spe_norm$sample_id)
##  [1] "ST_01" "ST_02" "ST_03" "ST_04" "ST_05" "ST_06" "ST_07" "ST_08" "ST_09"
## [10] "ST_10" "ST_11" "ST_12" "ST_13" "ST_14" "ST_15" "ST_16" "ST_17"
# Subset del SPE a una sola muestra
spe_04 <- filtered_spe_norm[, filtered_spe_norm$sample_id == "ST_04"]

cat("Muestra seleccionada:", "ST_04", "\n")
## Muestra seleccionada: ST_04
cat("Número de spots:", ncol(spe_04), "\n")
## Número de spots: 1336
cat("Barcodes duplicados:", sum(duplicated(colnames(spe_04))), "\n")
## Barcodes duplicados: 0
# Matriz de conteos
filtered_mat_04 <- assay(spe_04, "counts")
dim(filtered_mat_04)
## [1] 12161  1336
# Coordenadas de píxeles
coords <- spatialCoords(spe_04)
coords <- as.data.frame(coords)
colnames(coords)
## [1] "pxl_col_in_fullres" "pxl_row_in_fullres"
pos_04 <- data.frame(
  x = coords$pxl_col_in_fullres,
  y = coords$pxl_row_in_fullres
)

# Asignar los barcodes como rownames (únicos al ser una sola muestra)
rownames(pos_04) <- colnames(filtered_mat_04)

#comprobar que las dimensiones son correctas
all(colnames(filtered_mat_04) == rownames(pos_04))  # debe ser TRUE
## [1] TRUE
dim(filtered_mat_04)
## [1] 12161  1336
dim(pos_04)
## [1] 1336    2
ncol(filtered_mat_04)
## [1] 1336
nrow(pos_04)
## [1] 1336
#cargar los 
symbols_04 <- rowData(spe_04)$symbol

# Rellenar NAs con el Ensembl ID como fallback (igual que haces en genes_01)
symbols_04[is.na(symbols_04)] <- rownames(filtered_mat_04)[is.na(symbols_04)]

# Hay genes con símbolo duplicado — hacerlos únicos para evitar problemas
symbols_04 <- make.unique(symbols_04)

# Asignar como rownames
rownames(filtered_mat_04) <- symbols_04

###ST_16

# Ver las muestras disponibles en el objeto
unique(filtered_spe_norm$sample_id)
##  [1] "ST_01" "ST_02" "ST_03" "ST_04" "ST_05" "ST_06" "ST_07" "ST_08" "ST_09"
## [10] "ST_10" "ST_11" "ST_12" "ST_13" "ST_14" "ST_15" "ST_16" "ST_17"
# Subset del SPE a una sola muestra
spe_16 <- filtered_spe_norm[, filtered_spe_norm$sample_id == "ST_16"]

cat("Muestra seleccionada:", "ST_16", "\n")
## Muestra seleccionada: ST_16
cat("Número de spots:", ncol(spe_16), "\n")
## Número de spots: 1171
cat("Barcodes duplicados:", sum(duplicated(colnames(spe_16))), "\n")
## Barcodes duplicados: 0
# Matriz de conteos
filtered_mat_16 <- assay(spe_16, "counts")
dim(filtered_mat_16)
## [1] 12161  1171
# Coordenadas de píxeles
coords <- spatialCoords(spe_16)
coords <- as.data.frame(coords)
colnames(coords)
## [1] "pxl_col_in_fullres" "pxl_row_in_fullres"
pos_16 <- data.frame(
  x = coords$pxl_col_in_fullres,
  y = coords$pxl_row_in_fullres
)

# Asignar los barcodes como rownames (únicos al ser una sola muestra)
rownames(pos_16) <- colnames(filtered_mat_16)


all(colnames(filtered_mat_16) == rownames(pos_16))  # debe ser TRUE
## [1] TRUE
dim(filtered_mat_16)
## [1] 12161  1171
dim(pos_16)
## [1] 1171    2
ncol(filtered_mat_16)
## [1] 1171
nrow(pos_16)
## [1] 1171
symbols_16 <- rowData(spe_16)$symbol

# Rellenar NAs con el Ensembl ID como fallback (igual que haces en genes_01)
symbols_16[is.na(symbols_16)] <- rownames(filtered_mat_16)[is.na(symbols_16)]

# Hay genes con símbolo duplicado — hacerlos únicos para evitar problemas
symbols_16 <- make.unique(symbols_16)

# Asignar como rownames
rownames(filtered_mat_16) <- symbols_16

###ST_01

# Ver las muestras disponibles en el objeto
unique(filtered_spe_norm$sample_id)
##  [1] "ST_01" "ST_02" "ST_03" "ST_04" "ST_05" "ST_06" "ST_07" "ST_08" "ST_09"
## [10] "ST_10" "ST_11" "ST_12" "ST_13" "ST_14" "ST_15" "ST_16" "ST_17"
# Subset del SPE a una sola muestra
spe_01 <- filtered_spe_norm[, filtered_spe_norm$sample_id == "ST_01"]

cat("Muestra seleccionada:", "ST_01", "\n")
## Muestra seleccionada: ST_01
cat("Número de spots:", ncol(spe_01), "\n")
## Número de spots: 828
cat("Barcodes duplicados:", sum(duplicated(colnames(spe_01))), "\n")
## Barcodes duplicados: 0
# Matriz de conteos
filtered_mat_01 <- assay(spe_01, "counts")
dim(filtered_mat_01)
## [1] 12161   828
# Coordenadas de píxeles
coords <- spatialCoords(spe_01)
coords <- as.data.frame(coords)
colnames(coords)
## [1] "pxl_col_in_fullres" "pxl_row_in_fullres"
pos_01 <- data.frame(
  x = coords$pxl_col_in_fullres,
  y = coords$pxl_row_in_fullres
)

# Asignar los barcodes como rownames (únicos al ser una sola muestra)
rownames(pos_01) <- colnames(filtered_mat_01)


all(colnames(filtered_mat_01) == rownames(pos_01))  # debe ser TRUE
## [1] TRUE
dim(filtered_mat_01)
## [1] 12161   828
dim(pos_01)
## [1] 828   2
ncol(filtered_mat_01)
## [1] 828
nrow(pos_01)
## [1] 828
symbols_01 <- rowData(spe_01)$symbol

# Rellenar NAs con el Ensembl ID como fallback (igual que haces en genes_01)
symbols_01[is.na(symbols_01)] <- rownames(filtered_mat_01)[is.na(symbols_01)]

# Hay genes con símbolo duplicado — hacerlos únicos para evitar problemas
symbols_01 <- make.unique(symbols_01)

# Asignar como rownames
rownames(filtered_mat_01) <- symbols_01

Pipeline de STdeconvolve

Cleaning and feature selection

Primero se eliminan los barcodes con menos de 100 genes detectados y los genes con menos de 10 lecturas.

ST_04

counts_04 <- cleanCounts(filtered_mat_04, min.lib.size = 100, min.reads = 10, plot = TRUE)

Ahora se hace una selección de genes dispersos: que no estén en el 100% de las células pero sí en más del 5%. Se seleccionan los 1000 genes más dispersos (aunque pueden ser menos).

corpus_04 <- restrictCorpus(counts_04, removeAbove=1.0, removeBelow = 0.05, nTopOD = 1000, verbose = TRUE, plot = TRUE)
## Removing 78 genes present in 100% or more of pixels...
## 12025 genes remaining...
## Removing 134 genes present in 5% or less of pixels...
## 11891 genes remaining...
## Restricting to overdispersed genes with alpha = 0.05...
## Calculating variance fit ...
## Using gam with k=5...
## 291 overdispersed genes ...

##  Using top 1000 overdispersed genes.
##  number of top overdispersed genes available: 291

ST_16

counts_16 <- cleanCounts(filtered_mat_16, min.lib.size = 100, min.reads = 10, plot = TRUE)

corpus_16 <- restrictCorpus(counts_16, removeAbove=1.0, removeBelow = 0.05, nTopOD = 1000, verbose = TRUE, plot = TRUE)
## Removing 156 genes present in 100% or more of pixels...
## 11970 genes remaining...
## Removing 301 genes present in 5% or less of pixels...
## 11669 genes remaining...
## Restricting to overdispersed genes with alpha = 0.05...
## Calculating variance fit ...
## Using gam with k=5...
## 1264 overdispersed genes ...

##  Using top 1000 overdispersed genes.

ST_01

counts_01 <- cleanCounts(filtered_mat_01, min.lib.size = 100, min.reads = 10, plot = TRUE)

corpus_01 <- restrictCorpus(counts_01, removeAbove=1.0, removeBelow = 0.05, nTopOD = 1000, verbose = TRUE, plot = TRUE)
## Removing 112 genes present in 100% or more of pixels...
## 12029 genes remaining...
## Removing 122 genes present in 5% or less of pixels...
## 11907 genes remaining...
## Restricting to overdispersed genes with alpha = 0.05...
## Calculating variance fit ...
## Using gam with k=5...
## 604 overdispersed genes ...

##  Using top 1000 overdispersed genes.
##  number of top overdispersed genes available: 604

LDA

Se aplica el algoritmo de deconvolución

ST_04

## Note: the input corpus needs to be an integer count matrix of pixels x genes
ldas_04 <- fitLDA(t(as.matrix(corpus_04)), Ks = seq(5, 6, by = 1),
               perc.rare.thresh = 0.05,
               plot=TRUE,
               verbose=TRUE)
## Time to fit LDA models was 3.31 mins
## Computing perplexity for each fitted model...
## Time to compute perplexities was 0.02 mins
## Getting predicted cell-types at low proportions...
## Time to compute cell-types at low proportions was 0 mins
## Plotting...
## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = rareCtsAdj, x = K), col =
## "blue", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`
## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = perplexAdj, x = K), col =
## "red", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`
## Warning: The `size` argument of `element_line()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the STdeconvolve package.
##   Please report the issue at
##   <https://github.com/JEFworks-Lab/STdeconvolve/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

## select model with minimum perplexity
optLDA_04 <- optimalModel(models = ldas_04, opt = 6)

## extract pixel cell-type proportions (theta) and cell-type gene expression profiles (beta) for the given dataset
## we can also remove cell-types from pixels that contribute less than 5% of the pixel proportion
## and scale the deconvolved transcriptional profiles by 1000 
results_04 <- getBetaTheta(optLDA_04,
                        perc.filt = 0.05,
                        betaScale = 1000)
## Filtering out cell-types in pixels that contribute less than 0.05 of the pixel proportion.
deconProp_04 <- results_04$theta
deconGexp_04 <- results_04$beta

ST_16

## Note: the input corpus needs to be an integer count matrix of pixels x genes
ldas_16 <- fitLDA(t(as.matrix(corpus_16)), Ks = seq(11, 12, by = 1),
               perc.rare.thresh = 0.05,
               plot=TRUE,
               verbose=TRUE) 
## Time to fit LDA models was 46.79 mins
## Computing perplexity for each fitted model...
## Time to compute perplexities was 0.02 mins
## Getting predicted cell-types at low proportions...
## Time to compute cell-types at low proportions was 0 mins
## Plotting...
## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = rareCtsAdj, x = K), col =
## "blue", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`
## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = perplexAdj, x = K), col =
## "red", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`

## select model with minimum perplexity
optLDA_16 <- optimalModel(models = ldas_16, opt = 12)

## extract pixel cell-type proportions (theta) and cell-type gene expression profiles (beta) for the given dataset
## we can also remove cell-types from pixels that contribute less than 5% of the pixel proportion
## and scale the deconvolved transcriptional profiles by 1000 
results_16 <- getBetaTheta(optLDA_16,
                        perc.filt = 0.05,
                        betaScale = 1000)
## Filtering out cell-types in pixels that contribute less than 0.05 of the pixel proportion.
deconProp_16 <- results_16$theta
deconGexp_16 <- results_16$beta

ST_01

## Note: the input corpus needs to be an integer count matrix of pixels x genes
ldas_01 <- fitLDA(t(as.matrix(corpus_01)), Ks = seq(10, 11, by = 1),
               perc.rare.thresh = 0.05,
               plot=TRUE,
               verbose=TRUE)
## Time to fit LDA models was 15 mins
## Computing perplexity for each fitted model...
## Time to compute perplexities was 0.01 mins
## Getting predicted cell-types at low proportions...
## Time to compute cell-types at low proportions was 0 mins
## Plotting...
## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = rareCtsAdj, x = K), col =
## "blue", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`
## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(y = perplexAdj, x = K), col =
## "red", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `linewidth`

## select model with minimum perplexity
optLDA_01 <- optimalModel(models = ldas_01, opt = 11)

## extract pixel cell-type proportions (theta) and cell-type gene expression profiles (beta) for the given dataset
## we can also remove cell-types from pixels that contribute less than 5% of the pixel proportion
## and scale the deconvolved transcriptional profiles by 1000 
results_01 <- getBetaTheta(optLDA_01,
                        perc.filt = 0.05,
                        betaScale = 1000)
## Filtering out cell-types in pixels that contribute less than 0.05 of the pixel proportion.
deconProp_01 <- results_01$theta
deconGexp_01 <- results_01$beta

Proporción de tipos por píxeles

ST_04

plt <- vizAllTopics(theta = deconProp_04,
                   pos = pos_04,
                   r = 100,
                   lwd = 0,
                   showLegend = TRUE,
                   plotTitle = NA) +
  ggplot2::guides(fill=ggplot2::guide_legend(ncol=2)) +
  
  ## outer border
  ggplot2::annotate('rect',    xmin = min(pos_04$x) - 90,
    xmax = max(pos_04$x) + 90,
    ymin = min(pos_04$y) - 90,
    ymax = max(pos_04$y) + 90,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  
  ## remove the pixel "groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
  ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
  
  ggplot2::scale_y_reverse()
## Plotting scatterpies for 1336 pixels with 6 cell-types...this could take a while if the dataset is large.
plt

ps <- lapply(colnames(deconProp_04), function(celltype) {
  
  vizTopic(theta = deconProp_04, pos = pos_04, topic = celltype, plotTitle = paste0("X", celltype),
         size = 0.8, stroke = 0.3, alpha = 0.5,
         low = "white",
         high = "red") +
    
    ## remove the pixel "Groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
    ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
    
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.key.height = ggplot2::unit(0.4, "cm"),   
      legend.key.width  = ggplot2::unit(0.2, "cm"),   
      legend.text       = ggplot2::element_text(size = 5),  
      legend.title      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 6),  
      plot.title        = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7)   
    )+
  
  ggplot2::scale_y_reverse()
  
})
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  grobs = ps,
  layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2, 3),
                        c(4, 5, 6) )
)

#### ST_16

plt <- vizAllTopics(theta = deconProp_16,
                   pos = pos_16,
                   r = 100,
                   lwd = 0,
                   showLegend = TRUE,
                   plotTitle = NA) +
  ggplot2::guides(fill=ggplot2::guide_legend(ncol=2)) +
  
  ## outer border
  ggplot2::annotate('rect',    xmin = min(pos_16$x) - 90,
    xmax = max(pos_16$x) + 90,
    ymin = min(pos_16$y) - 90,
    ymax = max(pos_16$y) + 90,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  
  ## remove the pixel "groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
  ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
  
  ggplot2::scale_y_reverse()
## Plotting scatterpies for 1171 pixels with 12 cell-types...this could take a while if the dataset is large.
plt

ps <- lapply(colnames(deconProp_16), function(celltype) {
  
  vizTopic(theta = deconProp_16, pos = pos_16, topic = celltype, plotTitle = paste0("X", celltype),
         size = 0.8, stroke = 0.3, alpha = 0.5,
         low = "white",
         high = "red") +
    
    ## remove the pixel "Groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
    ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
    
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.key.height = ggplot2::unit(0.4, "cm"),   
      legend.key.width  = ggplot2::unit(0.2, "cm"),   
      legend.text       = ggplot2::element_text(size = 5),  
      legend.title      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 6),  
      plot.title        = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7)   
    )+
  
  ggplot2::scale_y_reverse()
  
})
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  grobs = ps,
  layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                        c(5, 6, 7, 8),
                        c(9, 10, 11, 12))
)

#### ST_01

plt <- vizAllTopics(theta = deconProp_01,
                   pos = pos_01,
                   r = 100,
                   lwd = 0,
                   showLegend = TRUE,
                   plotTitle = NA) +
  ggplot2::guides(fill=ggplot2::guide_legend(ncol=2)) +
  
  ## outer border
  ggplot2::annotate('rect',    xmin = min(pos_01$x) - 90,
    xmax = max(pos_01$x) + 90,
    ymin = min(pos_01$y) - 90,
    ymax = max(pos_01$y) + 90,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  
  ## remove the pixel "groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
  ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
  
  ggplot2::scale_y_reverse()
## Plotting scatterpies for 828 pixels with 11 cell-types...this could take a while if the dataset is large.
plt

ps <- lapply(colnames(deconProp_01), function(celltype) {
  
  vizTopic(theta = deconProp_01, pos = pos_01, topic = celltype, plotTitle = paste0("X", celltype),
         size = 0.8, stroke = 0.3, alpha = 0.5,
         low = "white",
         high = "red") +
    
    ## remove the pixel "Groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
    ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
    
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.key.height = ggplot2::unit(0.4, "cm"),   
      legend.key.width  = ggplot2::unit(0.2, "cm"),   
      legend.text       = ggplot2::element_text(size = 5),  
      legend.title      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 6),  
      plot.title        = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7)   
    )+
  
  ggplot2::scale_y_reverse()
  
})
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  grobs = ps,
  layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                        c(5, 6, 7, 8),
                        c(9, 10, 11, NA))
)

###Genes marcadores

ST_04

## first, combine the positions and the cleaned counts matrix
c_04 <- counts_04
df_04 <- merge(as.data.frame(pos_04), 
            as.data.frame(t(as.matrix(c_04))), 
            by = 0)

## collect the top genes for subsequent visualization
## he quitado el unlist que va por fuera del lapply para quedarme con las listas de los subtipos por separado

markerGenes_04 <- lapply(colnames(deconProp_04), function(celltype) {
  
  celltype <- as.numeric(celltype)
  ## highly expressed in cell-type of interest
  highgexp <- names(which(deconGexp_04[celltype,] > 3))
  ## high log2(fold-change) compared to other deconvolved cell-types
  log2fc <- sort(log2(deconGexp_04[celltype,highgexp]/colMeans(deconGexp_04[-celltype,highgexp])), decreasing=TRUE)
  markers <- names(log2fc)[1:30] ## label just the top gene
  ## collect name of top gene for each cell-type
  markers
})
  

## now visualize top genes for each deconvolved cell-type
ps <- lapply(markerGenes_04, function(marker) {
  vizGeneCounts(df = df_04,
              gene = marker[1],
              # groups = annot,
              # group_cols = rainbow(length(levels(annot))),
              size = 0.8, stroke = 0.05,
              plotTitle = marker[1],
              winsorize = 0.05,
              showLegend = TRUE) +
    
    ## remove the pixel "groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
    ggplot2::scale_y_reverse() +
    ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
    
    ## change some plot aesthetics
    ggplot2::theme(
        axis.text.x  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        axis.text.y  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),
        axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),
        plot.title   = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),   # <-- título pequeño
        legend.text  = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4, colour = "black"),
        legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4, colour = "black", angle = 90),
        legend.key.height = ggplot2::unit(0.3, "cm"),     # <-- barra más corta
        legend.key.width  = ggplot2::unit(0.15, "cm"),    # <-- barra más estrecha
        legend.margin     = ggplot2::margin(0, 0, 0, 0),  # <-- sin margen extra
        panel.background  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        panel.border = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        plot.background   = ggplot2::element_blank()
      ) +
    ggplot2::guides(fill = ggplot2::guide_colorbar(
      title = "Counts",
      title.position = "left",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      ticks.colour = "black",
      ticks.linewidth = 0.5,
      frame.colour= "black",
      frame.linewidth = 0.5,
      label.hjust = 0
       ))
})
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  grobs = ps,
  layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2, 3),
                        c(4, 5, 6))
)

ST_16

## first, combine the positions and the cleaned counts matrix
c_16 <- counts_16
df_16 <- merge(as.data.frame(pos_16), 
            as.data.frame(t(as.matrix(c_16))), 
            by = 0)

## collect the top genes for subsequent visualization
## he quitado el unlist que va por fuera del lapply para quedarme con las listas de los subtipos por separado

markerGenes_16 <- lapply(colnames(deconProp_16), function(celltype) {
  
  celltype <- as.numeric(celltype)
  ## highly expressed in cell-type of interest
  highgexp <- names(which(deconGexp_16[celltype,] > 3))
  ## high log2(fold-change) compared to other deconvolved cell-types
  log2fc <- sort(log2(deconGexp_16[celltype,highgexp]/colMeans(deconGexp_16[-celltype,highgexp])), decreasing=TRUE)
  markers <- names(log2fc)[1:30] ## label just the top gene
  ## collect name of top gene for each cell-type
  markers
})
  

## now visualize top genes for each deconvolved cell-type
ps <- lapply(markerGenes_16, function(marker) {
  vizGeneCounts(df = df_16,
              gene = marker[1],
              # groups = annot,
              # group_cols = rainbow(length(levels(annot))),
              size = 0.8, stroke = 0.05,
              plotTitle = marker[1],
              winsorize = 0.05,
              showLegend = TRUE) +
    
    ## remove the pixel "groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
    ggplot2::scale_y_reverse() +
    ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
    
    ## change some plot aesthetics
    ggplot2::theme(
        axis.text.x  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        axis.text.y  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),
        axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),
        plot.title   = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),   # <-- título pequeño
        legend.text  = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4, colour = "black"),
        legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4, colour = "black", angle = 90),
        legend.key.height = ggplot2::unit(0.3, "cm"),     # <-- barra más corta
        legend.key.width  = ggplot2::unit(0.15, "cm"),    # <-- barra más estrecha
        legend.margin     = ggplot2::margin(0, 0, 0, 0),  # <-- sin margen extra
        panel.background  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        panel.border = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        plot.background   = ggplot2::element_blank()
      ) +
    ggplot2::guides(fill = ggplot2::guide_colorbar(
      title = "Counts",
      title.position = "left",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      ticks.colour = "black",
      ticks.linewidth = 0.5,
      frame.colour= "black",
      frame.linewidth = 0.5,
      label.hjust = 0
       ))
})
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  grobs = ps,
  layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                        c(5, 6, 7, 8),
                        c(9, 10, 11, 12))
)

ST_01

## first, combine the positions and the cleaned counts matrix
c_01 <- counts_01
df_01 <- merge(as.data.frame(pos_01), 
            as.data.frame(t(as.matrix(c_01))), 
            by = 0)

## collect the top genes for subsequent visualization
## he quitado el unlist que va por fuera del lapply para quedarme con las listas de los subtipos por separado

markerGenes_01 <- lapply(colnames(deconProp_01), function(celltype) {
  
  celltype <- as.numeric(celltype)
  ## highly expressed in cell-type of interest
  highgexp <- names(which(deconGexp_01[celltype,] > 3))
  ## high log2(fold-change) compared to other deconvolved cell-types
  log2fc <- sort(log2(deconGexp_01[celltype,highgexp]/colMeans(deconGexp_01[-celltype,highgexp])), decreasing=TRUE)
  markers <- names(log2fc)[1:30] ## label just the top gene
  ## collect name of top gene for each cell-type
  markers
})
  

## now visualize top genes for each deconvolved cell-type
ps <- lapply(markerGenes_01, function(marker) {
  vizGeneCounts(df = df_01,
              gene = marker[1],
              # groups = annot,
              # group_cols = rainbow(length(levels(annot))),
              size = 0.8, stroke = 0.05,
              plotTitle = marker[1],
              winsorize = 0.05,
              showLegend = TRUE) +
    
    ## remove the pixel "groups", which is the color aesthetic for the pixel borders
    ggplot2::scale_y_reverse() +
    ggplot2::guides(colour = "none") +
    
    ## change some plot aesthetics
    ggplot2::theme(
        axis.text.x  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        axis.text.y  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),
        axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),
        plot.title   = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4),   # <-- título pequeño
        legend.text  = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4, colour = "black"),
        legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 4, colour = "black", angle = 90),
        legend.key.height = ggplot2::unit(0.3, "cm"),     # <-- barra más corta
        legend.key.width  = ggplot2::unit(0.15, "cm"),    # <-- barra más estrecha
        legend.margin     = ggplot2::margin(0, 0, 0, 0),  # <-- sin margen extra
        panel.background  = ggplot2::element_blank(),
        panel.border = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        plot.background   = ggplot2::element_blank()
      ) +
    ggplot2::guides(fill = ggplot2::guide_colorbar(
      title = "Counts",
      title.position = "left",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      ticks.colour = "black",
      ticks.linewidth = 0.5,
      frame.colour= "black",
      frame.linewidth = 0.5,
      label.hjust = 0
       ))
})
gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  grobs = ps,
  layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                        c(5, 6, 7, 8),
                        c(9, 10, 11, NA))
)
